SEARCH
Package io.github.projectunified.craftcommand.example.standalone

Class CalculatorCommand

java.lang.Object
io.github.projectunified.craftcommand.example.standalone.CalculatorCommand
public class CalculatorCommand extends Object
Comprehensive example command demonstrating all CraftCommand features.

Features demonstrated:

  • Command with name, aliases, description
  • Default action method
  • Subcommand on methods and nested classes
  • Optional parameters with default values
  • Greedy parameters (String and non-String types)
  • Name parameter name override
  • Suggest for tab completion (field and method)
  • Resolve for custom parameter resolution (implicit and explicit)
  • Min, Max validation annotations
  • ValidateWith custom validation
  • Nested subcommand classes
  • Multiple sender types

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    add(Object sender, int num1, int num2)
     
    void
    divide(Object sender, int num1, int num2)
     
    void
    echo(Object sender, String message)
    Demonstrates @Greedy on String (joins remaining args with spaces).
    void
    enumOp(Object sender, CalculatorCommand.MathOp op, int num1, int num2)
    Demonstrates enum parameter resolution (via registerProvider in CLIApp).
    void
    execute(Object sender, int num1, int num2)
    Default action: addition of two numbers.
    List<String>
    getModes(Object sender, String[] args, String current)
    Method-based suggestion provider with sender, args, and current input.
    void
    multiply(Object sender, int num1, int num2)
     
    void
    parse(Object sender, double value)
    Demonstrates @Greedy on non-String type (joins remaining args, then parses).
    void
    print(Object sender, String prefix, String text)
    Demonstrates @Optional with default value on String.
    void
    repeat(Object sender, String text, int count)
    Demonstrates @Optional with default value on int.
    CalculatorCommand.Point
    resolvePoint(double x, double y)
    Local resolver for Point type (multi-arg, width=2).
    CalculatorCommand.CustomSender
    resolveSender(Object sender)
    Local resolver for CustomSender type.
    void
    runMode(Object sender, String mode)
    Demonstrates method-based @Suggest with sender, args, current parameters.
    void
    runOp(Object sender, String op, int num1, int num2)
    Demonstrates field-based @Suggest for operator selection.
    void
    runPoint(Object sender, CalculatorCommand.Point pt)
    Demonstrates parameter-level @Resolve with named method.
    void
    sendMessage(Object sender, String target, String message)
    Demonstrates @Name to override parameter name in usage messages.
    void
    setCoordinate(Object sender, double x, double y, double z)
    Demonstrates @ValidateWith with custom validation method.
    void
    setLevel(Object sender, int level, double multiplier)
    Demonstrates @Min and @Max validation on numeric types.
    void
    subtract(Object sender, int num1, int num2)
     
    void
    sum(Object sender, int[] numbers)
    Demonstrates @Greedy on array type (creates array from remaining args).
    void
    validateCoordinate(double value)
    Validates coordinate is within bounds.
    void
    validateDivider(int num)
    Validates that divisor is not zero.
    void
    whoAmI(CalculatorCommand.CustomSender sender)
    Demonstrates sender-level @Resolve.

  • Field Details

    • operators

      public final List<String> operators
      Field-based suggestion provider for operator names.

  • Constructor Details

    • CalculatorCommand

      public CalculatorCommand()

  • Method Details

    • getModes

      public List<String> getModes(Object sender, String[] args, String current)
      Method-based suggestion provider with sender, args, and current input.
      Parameters:
      sender - the command sender
      args - the command arguments
      current - the current input being typed
      Returns:
      list of suggested modes

    • resolveSender

      public CalculatorCommand.CustomSender resolveSender(Object sender)
      Local resolver for CustomSender type.

    • resolvePoint

      public CalculatorCommand.Point resolvePoint(double x, double y)
      Local resolver for Point type (multi-arg, width=2).

    • validateDivider

      public void validateDivider(int num)
      Validates that divisor is not zero.

    • validateCoordinate

      public void validateCoordinate(double value)
      Validates coordinate is within bounds.

    • execute

      public void execute(Object sender, int num1, int num2)
      Default action: addition of two numbers. Usage: /calc <num1> <num2>

    • add

      public void add(Object sender, int num1, int num2)

      public void subtract(Object sender, int num1, int num2)

      Demonstrates field-based @Suggest for operator selection.

    • runMode

      public void runMode(Object sender, String mode)
      Demonstrates method-based @Suggest with sender, args, current parameters.

    • print

      public void print(Object sender, String prefix, String text)
      Demonstrates @Optional with default value on String.

    • repeat

      public void repeat(Object sender, String text, int count)
      Demonstrates @Optional with default value on int.

    • echo

      public void echo(Object sender, String message)
      Demonstrates @Greedy on String (joins remaining args with spaces).

    • parse

      public void parse(Object sender, double value)
      Demonstrates @Greedy on non-String type (joins remaining args, then parses).

    • sum

      public void sum(Object sender, int[] numbers)
      Demonstrates @Greedy on array type (creates array from remaining args).

    • sendMessage

      public void sendMessage(Object sender, String target, String message)
      Demonstrates @Name to override parameter name in usage messages.

    • runPoint

      public void runPoint(Object sender, CalculatorCommand.Point pt)
      Demonstrates parameter-level @Resolve with named method.

    • whoAmI

      public void whoAmI(CalculatorCommand.CustomSender sender)
      Demonstrates sender-level @Resolve.

    • setCoordinate

      Demonstrates @ValidateWith with custom validation method.

    • setLevel

      public void setLevel(Object sender, int level, double multiplier)
      Demonstrates @Min and @Max validation on numeric types.

    • enumOp

      public void enumOp(Object sender, CalculatorCommand.MathOp op, int num1, int num2)
      Demonstrates enum parameter resolution (via registerProvider in CLIApp).
